name: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model (DDPM)
training_approach: train neural network to reverse a gradual noising process via simplified variational bound
summary: DDPM revived and simplified diffusion-based generative models by showing that a straightforward training objective (predicting noise added at each step) could generate images rivaling GANs in quality without their training instability. Building on Sohl-Dickstein's 2015 thermodynamic diffusion framework, Ho, Jain, and Abbeel at UC Berkeley demonstrated that iterative denoising could produce high-fidelity samples, launching the diffusion model revolution that would power Stable Diffusion, DALL-E 2, and Midjourney.
